edges.description: Failure to assemble or stabilize the holoenzyme abolishes terminal electron transfer.
edges.source: COA8 Loss and Defective Complex IV Assembly
edges.target: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

edges.description: Energy failure in central white matter produces cavitating leukoencephalopathy.
edges.source: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

edges.description: Bioenergetic deficit in skeletal muscle produces mitochondrial myopathy with ragged-red fibers.
edges.source: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

edges.target: COA8 pathogenic variants causing COX deficiency

nodes.description: Biallelic loss-of-function COA8 variants remove a Complex IV assembly/stability factor, preventing maturation or maintenance of a functional COX holoenzyme.
nodes.id: COA8 Loss and Defective Complex IV Assembly

biological_processes: mitochondrial respiratory chain complex IV assembly

evidence.reference_title: Prominent muscle involvement in a familial form of mitochondrial disease due to a COA8 variant.
evidence.snippet: Causative mutations are mainly identified in structural COX subunits or in proteins involved in the maturation and assembly of the COX holocomplex.

nodes.description: A homozygous COA8 frameshift duplication identified in an Italian familial case of mitochondrial myopathy with COX deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Prominent muscle involvement in a familial form of mitochondrial disease due to a COA8 variant.
evidence.snippet: Whole Exome Sequencing analysis identified the novel homozygous COA8 defect c.170_173dupGACC, p.(Pro59fs) in the probands.

nodes.id: COA8 pathogenic variants causing COX deficiency

nodes.description: Loss of functional COX blocks electron transfer from cytochrome c to oxygen and proton pumping, collapsing oxidative ATP synthesis, with prominent central white matter and skeletal muscle involvement.
nodes.id: Impaired Terminal Electron Transfer and ATP Synthesis

biological_processes: mitochondrial electron transport, cytochrome c to oxygen

evidence.reference_title: Fatal infantile cardioencephalomyopathy with COX deficiency and mutations in SCO2, a COX assembly gene.
evidence.snippet: Mammalian cytochrome c oxidase (COX) catalyses the transfer of reducing equivalents from cytochrome c to molecular oxygen and pumps protons across the inner mitochondrial membrane.

nodes.description: Cavitating leukoencephalopathy, the classic CNS phenotype of COA8 deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Prominent muscle involvement in a familial form of mitochondrial disease due to a COA8 variant.
evidence.snippet: Loss-of-function COA8 mutations have been associated with cavitating leukoencephalopathy with COX deficiency in 9 reported individuals.

nodes.description: Ragged-red fibers and reduced COX staining on muscle biopsy.

evidence.reference_title: Prominent muscle involvement in a familial form of mitochondrial disease due to a COA8 variant.
evidence.snippet: Muscle biopsy displayed a diffuse reduction of COX activity staining and ragged-red fibers.
